title: Incomplete require statement causes compilation failure
reason: Rejected. `require(_holder != address(0));` is valid Solidity syntax and compiles normally.

title: Unchecked return values in restore function
reason: Rejected. Both ERC20 `transferFrom` calls in `restore()` are wrapped in `require`, so restoration cannot proceed if either payment fails.

title: Missing token existence validation in burn function
reason: Rejected. `burn()` uses a mapping, not an array; out-of-range IDs do not read garbage. For unset IDs `ownership[id]` is `address(0)`, so the owner check fails for any real caller.

title: Missing token existence validation in restore function
reason: Rejected. Mapping reads for unset IDs return `address(0)`, so `require(DEAD_ADDRESS == ownership[id])` fails. There is no out-of-bounds read primitive here.

title: Missing zero-address validation in constructor
reason: Rejected. This is deployer misconfiguration risk, not a permissionless protocol vulnerability.

title: Incorrect event parameter ordering in Transfer event
reason: Rejected. `emit Transfer(_from, _to, _tokenId)` uses the correct ERC721 argument order, and the candidate's duplicate-event rationale is not a vulnerability.

title: Curator role is not immutable and can be renounced
reason: Rejected. The current source has no `renounceOwnership()` path in the audited contract, and curator reassignment is an intentional privileged admin action rather than a security flaw.

title: Missing SafeMath for arithmetic operations
reason: Rejected. The contract uses Solidity `^0.8.11`, which has built-in checked arithmetic.

title: Token ID range not validated in approve function
reason: Rejected. `approve()` already checks `_tokenId < max`, and non-minted tokens cannot be approved because the authorization check fails when `ownership[_tokenId]` is zero.

title: Missing validation in tokenOfOwnerByIndex
reason: Merged into F-002. The function is indeed broken, but the reportable issue is the broader false `IERC721Enumerable` support claim and resulting integration breakage.

title: onERC721Received reverts instead of returning selector
reason: Merged into F-002. Reverting is only reportable here because `supportsInterface()` advertises receiver support.

title: Missing Return Values in ERC721 Interface Functions
reason: Rejected. The candidate misstates the code: `supportsInterface()` returns true for both `IERC721Enumerable` and `IERC721TokenReceiver`.
